Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And For $1945.50 Sanctions (Ccp 1985.3 (Gt), 1985.6 (F)
Off Calendar Feb-26-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Allem J. Kent, Esq., for Phillip Ryan, Defendant/Moving Party
Ryan J. Meckfessel, Esq., for Plaintiff/Opposing Party

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Philip Ryan's Motion For Order Quashing Subpoena For Production Of Business Records is denied. The subpoena is not for the purpose of the perpetuation of testimony or preservation of information pending appeal. The discovery sought by the Deposition Subpoenas for Production Of Business Records served upon First American Title Company is not for the purpose of seeking evidence for use in the event of further proceedings. The discovery sought goes to the enforcement of the judgment, which has not been stayed.
The court finds that the motion was brought without substantial justification. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $1,180, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
